{{神社| 名称=土崎神明社| 所在地=秋田県秋田市土崎港中央3丁目9-37| 祭神=[[天照大神]]| 社格=県社| 創建=[[1620年]]（[[元和]]6年）| 例祭=[[7月21日]]}}
'''土崎神明社'''（つちざきしんめいしゃ）は[[秋田県]][[秋田市]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 由緒 == 土崎神明社はもともと、[[1602年]]（[[慶長]]7年）に常陸から佐竹義宣とともに秋田にやってきた常陸国人川口惣治郎の氏神であった。現在の場所（[[湊城#出羽湊城|出羽湊城]]跡・現在の[[秋田市]][[土崎|土崎港]]）に移ったのは[[1620年]]（[[元和]]6年）のことで、川口惣治郎が土崎の肝煎として勧請したものである。以来土崎神明社は、土崎湊町の総鎮守として崇められてきた。
毎年[[7月20日]]、[[7月21日|21日]]の例祭の[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事|曳山行事]]は平成9年に[[国重要無形民俗文化財]]「」として指定されている。 曳山行事がいつ頃から行われるようになったかは定かではないが、寛政元年（1789年）に久保田藩の江戸藩邸御用商人であった津村淙庵が執筆したとされる「雪のふる道」には、四十もの山車が曳かれている様子が書かれている。このことから、遅くとも18世紀中頃までには神明社例祭として定着したのではないかと思われる。なお、土崎神明社の例祭は7月1日の 清祓いの儀式 に始まる一連の行事であるが、曳山行事はその終盤に当たる。また、曳山は大抵、単に「やま」と呼ばれることが多い。
== 祭神 == [[天照大神]]
== 祭事 == * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]]
== 境内外社 == * 菅原神社
== 文化財 == === 建造物 ===
=== その他 === * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[佐竹義和]]真筆懸額
== 名所・旧跡 ==
==年表== *[[1620年]] - 破却された[[湊城#出羽湊城|湊城]]址に川口惣治郎（弥左衛門とも）[[神明社]]を建立
== 交通 == === 鉄道 === * 最寄駅：[[土崎駅]]
=== バス === * 土崎駅入り口（[[秋田駅]]から将軍野線・寺内経由土崎線） ** [[秋田中央交通]]
=== その他 === * 駐車場 : 有り
== その他 ==
== 関連項目 == * [[神道]] ** [[神社一覧]] * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[土崎]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://shinnmeisya.web.infoseek.co.jp/ 土崎神明社] *[http://www.m-cross.co.jp/hikiyama/ 土崎港曳山まつり実行委員会]
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:秋田県の神社|つちさきしんめいしや]]

{{神社| 名称=土崎神明社| 所在地=秋田県秋田市土崎港中央3丁目9-37| 祭神=[[天照大神]]| 社格=県社| 創建=[[1620年]]（[[元和]]6年）| 例祭=[[7月21日]]}}
'''土崎神明社'''（つちざきしんめいしゃ）は[[秋田県]][[秋田市]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 由緒 == 土崎神明社はもともと、[[1602年]]（[[慶長]]7年）に常陸から佐竹義宣とともに秋田にやってきた常陸国人川口惣治郎の氏神であった。現在の場所（[[湊城#出羽湊城|出羽湊城]]跡・現在の[[秋田市]][[土崎|土崎港]]）に移ったのは[[1620年]]（[[元和]]6年）のことで、川口惣治郎が土崎の肝煎として勧請したものである。以来土崎神明社は、土崎湊町の総鎮守として崇められてきた。

== 祭神 == [[天照大神]]
== 祭事 == [[1月1日]]： 元旦祭、新年初神楽祈祷 [[1月15日]]： どんと祭 [[2月3日]]： 節分祭 [[2月27日]]： 祈年祭 [[春分の日]]： 鎮火祭 [[6月30日]]： 大祓 [[7月20日]][[7月21日|21日]]： 例祭（[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]]） [[11月23日]]： 新嘗祭 [[12月31日]]： 大祓
== 境内外社 == * 菅原神社（港天満宮）ほか
== 文化財 == * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[佐竹義和]]真筆懸額
==年表== *[[1620年]] - 破却された[[湊城#出羽湊城|湊城]]址に川口惣治郎（弥左衛門とも）[[神明社]]を建立
== 交通 == === 鉄道 === * 最寄駅：[[土崎駅]]
=== バス === * 土崎駅入り口（[[秋田駅]]から将軍野線・寺内経由土崎線） ** [[秋田中央交通]]
=== その他 === * 駐車場 : 有り

{{神社| 名称=土崎神明社| 所在地=秋田県秋田市土崎港中央3丁目9-37| 祭神=[[天照大神]]| 社格=県社| 創建=[[1620年]]（[[元和]]6年）| 例祭=[[7月21日]]}}
'''土崎神明社'''（つちざきしんめいしゃ）は[[秋田県]][[秋田市]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 由緒 == 土崎神明社はもともと、[[1602年]]（[[慶長]]7年）に常陸から佐竹義宣とともに秋田にやってきた常陸国人川口惣治郎の氏神であった。現在の場所（[[湊城#出羽湊城|出羽湊城]]跡・現在の[[秋田市]][[土崎|土崎港]]）に移ったのは[[1620年]]（[[元和]]6年）のことで、川口惣治郎が土崎の肝煎として勧請したものである。以来土崎神明社は、土崎湊町の総鎮守として崇められてきた。
毎年[[7月20日]]、[[7月21日|21日]]の例祭の[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事|曳山行事]]は平成9年に国[[重要文化財|重要無形民俗文化財]]として指定されている。 曳山行事がいつ頃から行われるようになったかは定かではないが、[[1789年]]（[[寛政]]1年）に久保田藩の江戸藩邸御用商人であった津村淙庵が執筆したとされる「雪のふる道」には、40もの山車が曳かれている様子が書かれている。このことから、遅くとも18世紀中頃までには神明社例祭として定着したのではないかと思われる。なお、土崎神明社の例祭は[[7月1日]]の 清祓いの儀式 に始まる一連の行事であるが、曳山行事はその終盤に当たる。また、曳山は大抵、単に「やま」と呼ばれることが多い。
== 祭神 == [[天照大神]]
== 祭事 == * [[1月1日]]： 元旦祭、新年初神楽祈祷 * [[1月15日]]： どんと祭 * [[2月3日]]： 節分祭 * [[2月27日]]： 祈年祭 * [[春分の日]]： 鎮火祭 * [[6月30日]]： 大祓 * [[7月20日]][[7月21日|21日]]： 例祭（[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]]） * [[11月23日]]： 新嘗祭 * [[12月31日]]： 大祓
== 境内外社 == * 菅原神社（港天満宮）ほか
== 文化財 == * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[佐竹義和]]真筆懸額
==年表== *[[1620年]] - 破却された[[湊城#出羽湊城|湊城]]址に川口惣治郎（弥左衛門とも）[[神明社]]を建立
== 交通 == === 鉄道 === * 最寄駅：[[土崎駅]]
=== バス === * 土崎駅入り口（[[秋田駅]]から将軍野線・寺内経由土崎線） ** [[秋田中央交通]]
=== その他 === * 駐車場 : 有り
== 関連項目 == * [[神道]] ** [[神社一覧]] * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[土崎]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://shinnmeisya.web.infoseek.co.jp/ 土崎神明社] *[http://www.m-cross.co.jp/hikiyama/ 土崎港曳山まつり実行委員会]
[[Category:秋田県の神社|つちさきしんめいしや]] [[Category:府県社|つちさきしんめいしや]]

{{神社| 名称=土崎神明社| 所在地=秋田県秋田市土崎港中央3丁目9-37| 祭神=[[天照大神]]| 社格=県社| 創建=[[1620年]]（[[元和]]6年）| 例祭=[[7月21日]]}}
'''土崎神明社'''（つちざきしんめいしゃ）は[[秋田県]][[秋田市]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 由緒 == 土崎神明社はもともと、[[1602年]]（[[慶長]]7年）に[[常陸国|常陸]]から[[佐竹義宣]]とともに秋田にやってきた常陸国人川口惣治郎の[[氏神]]であった。現在の場所（[[湊城#出羽湊城|出羽湊城]]跡・現在の[[秋田市]][[土崎|土崎港]]）に移ったのは[[1620年]]（[[元和]]6年）のことで、川口惣治郎が土崎の肝煎として勧請したものである。以来土崎神明社は、土崎湊町の総[[鎮守神|鎮守]]として崇められてきた。
毎年[[7月20日]]、[[7月21日|21日]]の例祭の[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事|曳山行事]]は1997年（平成9年）に国の<!-- [[重要文化財#日本におけるその他の国指定文化財|重要無形民俗文化財]] -->[[重要無形民俗文化財]]として指定されている。
== 祭神 == [[天照大神]]
== 祭事 == * [[1月1日]]： 元旦祭、新年初神楽祈祷 * [[1月15日]]： どんと祭 * [[2月3日]]： 節分祭 * [[2月27日]]： 祈年祭 * [[春分の日]]： 鎮火祭 * [[6月30日]]： 大祓 * [[7月20日]][[7月21日|21日]]： 例祭（[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]]） * [[11月23日]]： 新嘗祭 * [[12月31日]]： 大祓
== 境内外社 == * 菅原神社（港天満宮）ほか
== 文化財 == * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[佐竹義和]]真筆懸額

[[Category:秋田県の神社|つちさきしんめいしや]] [[Category:府県社|つちさきしんめいしや]] [[Category:神明神社|つちさきしんめいしや]] [[Category:秋田市|つちさきしんめいしや]]

{{神社| 名称=土崎神明社| 所在地=秋田県秋田市土崎港中央3丁目9-37| 祭神=[[天照大神]]| 社格=県社| 創建=[[1620年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年）| 例祭=[[7月21日]]}}
'''土崎神明社'''（つちざきしんめいしゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[秋田市]]にある[[神社]]である。
== 由緒 == 土崎神明社はもともと、[[1602年]]（[[慶長]]7年）に[[常陸国|常陸]]から[[佐竹義宣 (右京大夫)|佐竹義宣]]とともに秋田にやってきた常陸国人川口惣治郎の[[氏神]]であった。現在の場所（[[湊城#出羽湊城|出羽湊城]]跡・現在の[[秋田市]][[土崎|土崎港]]）に移ったのは[[1620年]]（[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年）のことで、川口惣治郎が土崎の肝煎として勧請したものである。以来土崎神明社は、土崎湊町の総[[鎮守神|鎮守]]として崇められてきた。
毎年[[7月20日]]、[[7月21日|21日]]の例祭の[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事|曳山行事]]は1997年（平成9年）に国の<!-- [[重要文化財#日本におけるその他の国指定文化財|重要無形民俗文化財]] -->[[重要無形民俗文化財]]として指定されている。
== 祭神 == [[天照大神]]
== 祭事 == * [[1月1日]]： 元旦祭、新年初神楽祈祷 * [[1月15日]]： どんと祭 * [[2月3日]]： 節分祭 * [[2月27日]]： 祈年祭 * [[春分の日]]： 鎮火祭 * [[6月30日]]： 大祓 * [[7月20日]][[7月21日|21日]]： 例祭（[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]]） * [[11月23日]]： 新嘗祭 * [[12月31日]]： 大祓
== 境内外社 == * 菅原神社（港天満宮）ほか
== 文化財 == * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[佐竹義和]]真筆懸額
==年表== *[[1620年]] - 破却された[[湊城#出羽湊城|湊城]]址に川口惣治郎（弥左衛門とも）'''土崎神明社'''を建立
== 交通 == === 鉄道 === * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]] [[奥羽本線]] [[土崎駅]]から徒歩2分
=== バス === * 土崎駅入口（[[秋田駅]]から新国道経由の各線(セリオン・フェリーターミナル行きを除く)・寺内経由土崎線）から徒歩4分 ** [[秋田中央交通]]
=== その他 === * 駐車場 : 有り

== 外部リンク == *[http://shinnmeisya.web.infoseek.co.jp/ 土崎神明社] *[http://www.m-cross.co.jp/hikiyama/ 土崎港曳山まつり実行委員会]

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=土崎神明社| 画像=[[画像:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿| 所在地=秋田県秋田市土崎港中央3丁目9-37| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=[[天照大神]]| 社格=旧県社| 創建=[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]）| 本殿=[[神明造]]銅板葺| 例祭=[[7月21日]]}} '''土崎神明社'''（つちざきしんめいしゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[秋田市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 由緒 == 土崎神明社はもともと、[[慶長]]7年（[[1602年]]）に[[常陸国|常陸]]から[[佐竹義宣 (右京大夫)|佐竹義宣]]とともに秋田にやってきた常陸国人川口惣治郎の[[氏神]]であった。現在の場所（[[湊城#出羽湊城|出羽湊城跡]]・現在の[[秋田市]][[土崎|土崎港]]）に移ったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]）のことで、川口惣治郎が土崎の肝煎として勧請したものである。以来土崎神明社は、土崎湊町の総[[鎮守神|鎮守]]として崇められてきた。[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）に県社に列し、戦後は[[神社本庁]]に参加。 毎年[[7月20日]]、[[7月21日|21日]]の例祭の[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事|曳山行事]]は[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]として指定されている。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神]] == 祭事 == * [[1月1日]]： 元旦祭、新年初神楽祈祷 * [[1月15日]]： どんと祭 * [[2月3日]]： 節分祭 * [[2月27日]]： 祈年祭 * [[春分の日]]： 鎮火祭 * [[6月30日]]： 大祓 * [[7月20日]][[7月21日|21日]]： 例祭（[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]]） :曳山行事がいつ頃から行われるようになったかは定かではないが、[[寛政]]元年（[[1789年]]）に[[久保田藩]]の江戸藩邸御用商人であった[[津村淙庵]]が執筆したとされる『雪のふる道』には、40もの山車が曳かれている様子が書かれている。このことから、遅くとも[[18世紀]]中頃までには神明社例祭として定着したのではないかと思われる。なお、土崎神明社の例祭は[[7月1日]]の 清祓いの儀式 に始まる一連の行事であるが、曳山行事はその終盤に当たる。また、曳山は大抵、単に「やま」と呼ばれることが多い。 * [[11月23日]]： 新嘗祭 * [[12月31日]]： 大祓 == 境内外社 == * 大国主神社 * 菅原神社（港天満宮） * 西宮神社 * 日影稲荷神社 * 番匠神社 == 文化財 == * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[佐竹義和]]真筆懸額 ==年表== *元和6年（1620年） - 破却された出羽湊城址に川口惣治郎（弥左衛門とも）'''土崎神明社'''を建立 == 交通 == === 鉄道 === * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]] [[奥羽本線]] [[土崎駅]]から徒歩2分 === バス === * 土崎駅入口（[[秋田駅]]から新国道経由の各線(セリオン・フェリーターミナル行きを除く)・寺内経由土崎線）から徒歩4分 ** [[秋田中央交通]] === その他 === * 駐車場 : 有り == 関連項目 == * [[神道]] ** [[神社一覧]] * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[土崎]] * [[土崎街区公園]] == 外部リンク == *[http://shinnmeisya.web.infoseek.co.jp/ 土崎神明社] *[http://www.m-cross.co.jp/hikiyama/ 土崎港曳山まつり実行委員会] {{DEFAULTSORT:つちさきしんめいしや}} [[Category:秋田県の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:秋田市]] {{神道 横}}

revision.comment: 復帰分を「土崎神明社祭の曳山行事」に丸投げ
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=土崎神明社| 画像=[[画像:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿| 所在地=秋田県秋田市土崎港中央3丁目9-37| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=[[天照大神]]| 社格=旧県社| 創建=[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]）| 本殿=[[神明造]]銅板葺| 例祭=[[7月21日]]}} '''土崎神明社'''（つちざきしんめいしゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[秋田市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 由緒 == 土崎神明社はもともと、[[慶長]]7年（[[1602年]]）に[[常陸国|常陸]]から[[佐竹義宣 (右京大夫)|佐竹義宣]]とともに秋田にやってきた常陸国人川口惣治郎の[[氏神]]であった。現在の場所（[[湊城#出羽湊城|出羽湊城跡]]・現在の[[秋田市]][[土崎|土崎港]]）に移ったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]）のことで、川口惣治郎が土崎の肝煎として勧請したものである。以来土崎神明社は、土崎湊町の総[[鎮守神|鎮守]]として崇められてきた。[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）に県社に列し、戦後は[[神社本庁]]に参加。 毎年[[7月20日]]、[[7月21日|21日]]の例祭の[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事|曳山行事]]は[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]として指定されている。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神]] == 祭事 == * [[1月1日]]： 元旦祭、新年初神楽祈祷 * [[1月15日]]： どんと祭 * [[2月3日]]： 節分祭 * [[2月27日]]： 祈年祭 * [[春分の日]]： 鎮火祭 * [[6月30日]]： 大祓 * [[7月21日]]： 例祭 :例祭にあわせて、前日（[[7月20日|20日]]）から曳山行事が行われる（詳しくは[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]]参照）。 * [[11月23日]]： 新嘗祭 * [[12月31日]]： 大祓 == 境内外社 == * 大国主神社 * 菅原神社（港天満宮） * 西宮神社 * 日影稲荷神社 * 番匠神社 == 文化財 == * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[佐竹義和]]真筆懸額 ==年表== *元和6年（1620年） - 破却された出羽湊城址に川口惣治郎（弥左衛門とも）'''土崎神明社'''を建立 == 交通 == === 鉄道 === * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]] [[奥羽本線]] [[土崎駅]]から徒歩2分 === バス === * 土崎駅入口（[[秋田駅]]から新国道経由の各線(セリオン・フェリーターミナル行きを除く)・寺内経由土崎線）から徒歩4分 ** [[秋田中央交通]] === その他 === * 駐車場 : 有り == 関連項目 == * [[神道]] ** [[神社一覧]] * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[土崎]] * [[土崎街区公園]] == 外部リンク == *[http://shinnmeisya.web.infoseek.co.jp/ 土崎神明社] *[http://www.m-cross.co.jp/hikiyama/ 土崎港曳山まつり実行委員会] {{DEFAULTSORT:つちさきしんめいしや}} [[Category:秋田県の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:秋田市]] {{神道 横}}

{{神社| 名称=土崎神明社| 画像=[[画像:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿| 所在地=秋田県秋田市土崎港中央3丁目9-37| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=[[天照大神]]| 社格=旧県社| 創建=[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]）| 本殿=[[神明造]]銅板葺| 例祭=[[7月21日]]}} '''土崎神明社'''（つちざきしんめいしゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[秋田市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 由緒 == 土崎神明社はもともと、[[慶長]]7年（[[1602年]]）に[[常陸国|常陸]]から[[佐竹義宣 (右京大夫)|佐竹義宣]]とともに秋田にやってきた常陸国人川口惣治郎の[[氏神]]であった。現在の場所（[[湊城#出羽湊城|出羽湊城跡]]・現在の[[秋田市]][[土崎港]]）に移ったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]）のことで、川口惣治郎が土崎の肝煎として勧請したものである。以来土崎神明社は、土崎湊町の総[[鎮守神|鎮守]]として崇められてきた。[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）に県社に列し、戦後は[[神社本庁]]に参加。 毎年[[7月20日]]、[[7月21日|21日]]の例祭の[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事|曳山行事]]は[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]として指定されている。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神]] == 祭事 == * [[1月1日]]： 元旦祭、新年初神楽祈祷 * [[1月15日]]： どんと祭 * [[2月3日]]： 節分祭 * [[2月27日]]： 祈年祭 * [[春分の日]]： 鎮火祭 * [[6月30日]]： 大祓 * [[7月21日]]： 例祭 :例祭にあわせて、前日（[[7月20日|20日]]）から曳山行事が行われる（詳しくは[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]]参照）。 * [[11月23日]]： 新嘗祭 * [[12月31日]]： 大祓 == 境内外社 == * 大国主神社 * 菅原神社（港天満宮） * 西宮神社 * 日影稲荷神社 * 番匠神社 == 文化財 == * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[佐竹義和]]真筆懸額 ==年表== *元和6年（1620年） - 破却された出羽湊城址に川口惣治郎（弥左衛門とも）'''土崎神明社'''を建立 == 交通 == === 鉄道 === * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]] [[奥羽本線]] [[土崎駅]]から徒歩2分 === バス === * 土崎駅入口（[[秋田駅]]から新国道経由の各線(セリオン・フェリーターミナル行きを除く)・寺内経由土崎線）から徒歩4分 ** [[秋田中央交通]] === その他 === * 駐車場 : 有り == 関連項目 == * [[神道]] ** [[神社一覧]] * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[土崎]] * [[土崎街区公園]] == 外部リンク == *[http://shinnmeisya.web.infoseek.co.jp/ 土崎神明社] *[http://www.m-cross.co.jp/hikiyama/ 土崎港曳山まつり実行委員会] {{DEFAULTSORT:つちさきしんめいしや}} [[Category:秋田県の神社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:秋田市]] {{神道 横}}

{{神社| 名称=土崎神明社| 画像=[[画像:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|拝殿]]

{{神社| 名称=土崎神明社| 画像=[[ファイル:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿| 所在地=秋田県秋田市土崎港中央3丁目9-37| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=[[天照大神]]| 社格=旧県社| 創建=[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]）| 本殿=[[神明造]]銅板葺| 例祭=[[7月21日]]}} '''土崎神明社'''（つちざきしんめいしゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[秋田市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 由緒 == 土崎神明社はもともと、[[慶長]]7年（[[1602年]]）に[[常陸国|常陸]]から[[佐竹義宣 (右京大夫)|佐竹義宣]]とともに秋田にやってきた常陸国人川口惣治郎の[[氏神]]であった。現在の場所（[[湊城|出羽湊城跡]]・現在の[[秋田市]][[土崎港]]）に移ったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]）のことで、川口惣治郎が土崎の肝煎として勧請したものである。以来土崎神明社は、土崎湊町の総[[鎮守神|鎮守]]として崇められてきた。[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）に県社に列し、戦後は[[神社本庁]]に参加。 毎年[[7月20日]]、[[7月21日|21日]]の例祭の[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事|曳山行事]]は[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]として指定されている。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神]] == 祭事 == * [[1月1日]]： 元旦祭、新年初神楽祈祷 * [[1月15日]]： どんと祭 * [[2月3日]]： 節分祭 * [[2月27日]]： 祈年祭 * [[春分の日]]： 鎮火祭 * [[6月30日]]： 大祓 * [[7月21日]]： 例祭 :例祭にあわせて、前日（[[7月20日|20日]]）から曳山行事が行われる（詳しくは[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]]参照）。 * [[11月23日]]： 新嘗祭 * [[12月31日]]： 大祓 == 境内外社 == * 大国主神社 * 菅原神社（港天満宮） * 西宮神社 * 日影稲荷神社 * 番匠神社 == 文化財 == * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[佐竹義和]]真筆懸額 ==年表== *元和6年（1620年） - 破却された出羽湊城址に川口惣治郎（弥左衛門とも）'''土崎神明社'''を建立 == 交通 == === 鉄道 === * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]] [[奥羽本線]] [[土崎駅]]から徒歩2分 === バス === * 土崎駅入口（[[秋田駅]]から新国道経由の各線(セリオン・フェリーターミナル行きを除く)・寺内経由土崎線）から徒歩4分 ** [[秋田中央交通]] === その他 === * 駐車場 : 有り == 参考文献 == * 土崎港町・秋田市合併五十周年記念誌編纂委員会『土崎の史誌－土崎港町・秋田市合併五十周年記念誌』　土崎史談会、1992年 * 秋田魁新報社編『秋田大百科事典』　秋田魁新報社、1981年、ISBN 4870200074 == 関連項目 == * [[神道]] ** [[神社一覧]] * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[土崎]] * [[土崎街区公園]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ 土崎神明社] *[http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ 土崎港曳山まつり実行委員会] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つちさきしんめいしや}} [[Category:秋田県の神社]] [[Category:秋田県の旧県社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:秋田市の建築物]] [[Category:秋田市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=土崎神明社 | 画像=[[ファイル:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[秋田県]][[秋田市]]土崎港中央3丁目9-37 | 位置={{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=土崎神明社}} | 祭神=[[天照大神]] | 社格=旧県社 | 創建=[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]） | 本殿=[[神明造]]銅板葺 | 例祭=[[7月21日]] | 地図={{Location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=土崎神明社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''土崎神明社'''（つちざきしんめいしゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[秋田市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 由緒 == 土崎神明社はもともと、[[慶長]]7年（[[1602年]]）に[[常陸国|常陸]]から[[佐竹義宣 (右京大夫)|佐竹義宣]]とともに秋田にやってきた常陸国人川口惣治郎の[[氏神]]であった。現在の場所（[[湊城|出羽湊城跡]]・現在の[[秋田市]][[土崎港]]）に移ったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]）のことで、川口惣治郎が土崎の肝煎として勧請したものである。以来土崎神明社は、土崎湊町の総[[鎮守神|鎮守]]として崇められてきた。[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）に県社に列し、戦後は[[神社本庁]]に参加。 毎年[[7月20日]]、[[7月21日|21日]]の例祭の[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事|曳山行事]]は[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]として指定されている。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神]] == 祭事 == * [[1月1日]]： 元旦祭、新年初神楽祈祷 * [[1月15日]]： どんと祭 * [[2月3日]]： 節分祭 * [[2月27日]]： 祈年祭 * [[春分の日]]： 鎮火祭 * [[6月30日]]： 大祓 * [[7月21日]]： 例祭 :例祭にあわせて、前日（[[7月20日|20日]]）から曳山行事が行われる（詳しくは[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]]参照）。 * [[11月23日]]： 新嘗祭 * [[12月31日]]： 大祓 == 境内外社 == * 大国主神社 * 菅原神社（港天満宮） * 西宮神社 * 日影稲荷神社 * 番匠神社 == 文化財 == * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[佐竹義和]]真筆懸額 ==年表== *[[元和]]6年（[[1620年]]） - 破却された出羽湊城址に川口惣治郎（弥左衛門とも）'''土崎神明社'''を建立する。 == 交通 == === 鉄道 === * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]] [[奥羽本線]] [[土崎駅]]から徒歩2分 === バス === * 土崎駅入口（[[秋田駅]]から新国道経由の各線(セリオン・フェリーターミナル行きを除く)・寺内経由土崎線）から徒歩4分 ** [[秋田中央交通]] === その他 === * 駐車場 : 有り == 参考文献 == {{No footnotes|date=2020年5月|section=1}} * 土崎港町・秋田市合併五十周年記念誌編纂委員会『土崎の史誌－土崎港町・秋田市合併五十周年記念誌』　土崎史談会、1992年 * 秋田魁新報社編『秋田大百科事典』　秋田魁新報社、1981年、ISBN 4870200074 == 関連項目 == * [[神道]] ** [[神社一覧]] * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[土崎]] * [[土崎街区公園]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ 土崎神明社] *[http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ 土崎港曳山まつり実行委員会] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つちさきしんめいしや}} [[Category:秋田県の神社]] [[Category:秋田県の旧県社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:秋田市の建築物]] [[Category:秋田市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha | image = [[File:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | address = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (annual festival) | map = {{Location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's annual festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == Deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == Festivals == * **January 1**: New Year's Festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * **January 15**: ''Donto'' Festival * **February 3**: Setsubun Festival * **February 27**: Harvest Prayer Festival (''Kinensai'') * **Vernal Equinox Day**: Fire-Prevention Festival (''Chinka-sai'') * **June 30**: Great Purification (''Oharae'') * **July 21**: Annual Grand Festival – Includes the [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * **November 23**: Harvest Festival (''Niinamesai'') * **December 31**: Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** * **Sugawara Shrine** (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * **Nishinomiya Shrine** * **Hikage Inari Shrine** * **Banjō Shrine** == Cultural Properties == * **Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Festival's Hikiyama Procession** – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * **Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga** – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * **1620 (Genna 6)**: Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama Festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita City]] [[Category:History of Akita City]]

via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama Festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita City]] [[Category:History of Akita City]] [[Category:original pages]]

Worship Hall | address = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (annual festival) | map = {{Location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's annual festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == Deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == Festivals == * **January 1**: New Year's Festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * **January 15**: ''Donto'' Festival * **February 3**: Setsubun Festival * **February 27**: Harvest Prayer Festival (''Kinensai'') * **Vernal Equinox Day**: Fire-Prevention Festival (''Chinka-sai'') * **June 30**: Great Purification (''Oharae'') * **July 21**: Annual Grand Festival – Includes the [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * **November 23**: Harvest Festival (''Niinamesai'') * **December 31**: Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** * **Sugawara Shrine** (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * **Nishinomiya Shrine** * **Hikage Inari Shrine** * **Banjō Shrine** == Cultural Properties == * **Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Festival's Hikiyama Procession** – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * **Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga** – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * **1620 (Genna 6)**: Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Worship Hall | address = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (annual festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's annual festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * **January 1**: New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * **January 15**: ''Donto'' festival * **February 3**: Setsubun festival * **February 27**: Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * **Vernal Equinox Day**: Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * **June 30**: Great Purification (''Oharae'') * **July 21**: annual Grand festival – Includes the [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * **November 23**: Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * **December 31**: Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** * **Sugawara Shrine** (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * **Nishinomiya Shrine** * **Hikage Inari Shrine** * **Banjō Shrine** == Cultural Properties == * **Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession** – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * **Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga** – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * **1620 (Genna 6)**: Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita City]] [[Category:History of Akita City]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha | image = [[File:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * **January 1**: New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * **January 15**: ''Donto'' festival * **February 3**: Setsubun festival * **February 27**: Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * **Vernal Equinox Day**: Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * **June 30**: Great Purification (''Oharae'') * **July 21**: annual Grand festival – Includes the [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * **November 23**: Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * **December 31**: Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** * **Sugawara Shrine** (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * **Nishinomiya Shrine** * **Hikage Inari Shrine** * **Banjō Shrine** == Cultural Properties == * **Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession** – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * **Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga** – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * **1620 (Genna 6)**: Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha | image = [[File:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * **January 1**: New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * **January 15**: ''Donto'' festival * **February 3**: Setsubun festival * **February 27**: Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * **Vernal Equinox Day**: Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * **June 30**: Great Purification (''Oharae'') * **July 21**: annual Grand festival – Includes the [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * **November 23**: Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * **December 31**: Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** * **Sugawara Shrine** (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * **Nishinomiya Shrine** * **Hikage Inari Shrine** * **Banjō Shrine** == Cultural Properties == * **Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession** – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * **Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga** – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * **1620 (Genna 6)**: Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * **January 1**: New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * **January 15**: ''Donto'' festival * **February 3**: Setsubun festival * **February 27**: Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * **Vernal Equinox Day**: Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * **June 30**: Great Purification (''Oharae'') * **July 21**: annual Grand festival – Includes the [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * **November 23**: Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * **December 31**: Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** * **Sugawara Shrine** (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * **Nishinomiya Shrine** * **Hikage Inari Shrine** * **Banjō Shrine** == Cultural Properties == * **Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession** – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * **Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga** – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * **1620 (Genna 6)**: Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * **January 1**: New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * **January 15**: ''Donto'' festival * **February 3**: Setsubun festival * **February 27**: Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * **Vernal Equinox Day**: Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * **June 30**: Great Purification (''Oharae'') * **July 21**: annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * **November 23**: Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * **December 31**: Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Ōkuninushi Shrine** * **Sugawara Shrine** (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * **Nishinomiya Shrine** * **Hikage Inari Shrine** * **Banjō Shrine** == Cultural Properties == * **Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession** – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * **Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga** – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * **1620 (Genna 6)**: Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ** [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita City]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita City]] It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] *
