Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074.== See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita City]] It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita City]] Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita City]] [[Category:History of Akita City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:ophans as of March 31]] }} [[sn:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha]] [[ja:土崎神明社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha]][[Category:qq]]

{{神社 | 名称=土崎神明社 | 画像=[[ファイル:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|拝殿]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[秋田県]][[秋田市]]土崎港中央3丁目9-37 | 位置={{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=土崎神明社}} | 祭神=[[天照大神]] | 社格=旧県社 | 創建=[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]） | 本殿=[[神明造]]銅板葺 | 例祭=[[7月21日]] | 地図={{Location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=土崎神明社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''土崎神明社'''（つちざきしんめいしゃ）は、[[秋田県]][[秋田市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 由緒 == 土崎神明社はもともと、[[慶長]]7年（[[1602年]]）に[[常陸国|常陸]]から[[佐竹義宣 (右京大夫)|佐竹義宣]]とともに秋田にやってきた常陸国人川口惣治郎の[[氏神]]であった。現在の場所（[[湊城|出羽湊城跡]]・現在の[[秋田市]][[土崎港]]）に移ったのは[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]）のことで、川口惣治郎が土崎の肝煎として勧請したものである。以来土崎神明社は、土崎湊町の総[[鎮守神|鎮守]]として崇められてきた。[[明治]]14年（[[1881年]]）に県社に列し、戦後は[[神社本庁]]に参加。 毎年[[7月20日]]、[[7月21日|21日]]の例祭の[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事|曳山行事]]は[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]として指定されている。 == 祭神 == [[天照大神]] == 祭事 == * [[1月1日]]： 元旦祭、新年初神楽祈祷 * [[1月15日]]： どんと祭 * [[2月3日]]： 節分祭 * [[2月27日]]： 祈年祭 * [[春分の日]]： 鎮火祭 * [[6月30日]]： 大祓 * [[7月21日]]： 例祭 :例祭にあわせて、前日（[[7月20日|20日]]）から曳山行事が行われる（詳しくは[[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]]参照）。 * [[11月23日]]： 新嘗祭 * [[12月31日]]： 大祓 == 境内外社 == * 大国主神社 * 菅原神社（港天満宮） * 西宮神社 * 日影稲荷神社 * 番匠神社 == 文化財 == * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[佐竹義和]]真筆懸額 ==年表== *[[元和 (日本)|元和]]6年（[[1620年]]） - 破却された出羽湊城址に川口惣治郎（弥左衛門とも）'''土崎神明社'''を建立する。 == 交通 == === 鉄道 === * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]] [[奥羽本線]] [[土崎駅]]から徒歩2分 === バス === * 土崎駅入口（[[秋田駅]]から新国道経由の各線(セリオン・フェリーターミナル行きを除く)・寺内経由土崎線）から徒歩4分 ** [[秋田中央交通]] === その他 === * 駐車場 : 有り == 参考文献 == {{No footnotes|date=2020年5月|section=1}} * 土崎港町・秋田市合併五十周年記念誌編纂委員会『土崎の史誌－土崎港町・秋田市合併五十周年記念誌』　土崎史談会、1992年 * 秋田魁新報社編『秋田大百科事典』　秋田魁新報社、1981年、ISBN 4870200074 == 関連項目 == * [[神道]] ** [[神社一覧]] * [[土崎神明社祭の曳山行事]] * [[土崎]] * [[土崎街区公園]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ 土崎神明社] *[http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ 土崎港曳山まつり実行委員会] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つちさきしんめいしや}} [[Category:秋田県の神社]] [[Category:秋田県の旧県社]] [[Category:神明神社]] [[Category:秋田市の建築物]] [[Category:秋田市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[土崎神明社]] to [[Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita City]] [[Category:History of Akita City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[sn:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha]] [[ja:土崎神明社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} {{translated page|ja|土崎神明社|version=89182105|comment=Imported full ja history}}

* '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|土崎神明社|version=89182105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita City]] [[Category:History of Akita City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[sn:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha]] [[ja:土崎神明社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita (city)]] [[Category:秋田市の歴史]]

* '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[:en:1602|1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|土崎神明社|version=89182105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:History of Akita City]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:orphaned pages as of April 8]] }} [[sn:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha]] [[ja:土崎神明社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita (city)]] [[Category:History of Akita, Akita]] [[Category:秋田県の旧県社]] {{Commons category|Tsuchizaki Shinmei-sha}}

Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[:en:1602|1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1881|1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[:en:1997|1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|土崎神明社|version=89182105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha]] [[ja:土崎神明社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita (city)]] [[Category:History of Akita, Akita]] [[Category:秋田県の旧県社]] {{Commons category|Tsuchizaki Shinmei-sha}}

Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[JR East]] [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[:en:1602|1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1881|1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[:en:1997|1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|土崎神明社|version=89182105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha]] [[ja:土崎神明社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita (city)]] [[Category:History of Akita, Akita]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Akita Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tsuchizaki Shinmei-sha}}

It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1881|1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[:en:July 21|July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[:en:1997|1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ōu Main Line]] [[Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha]] [[ja:土崎神明社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita (city)]] [[Category:History of Akita, Akita]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Akita Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tsuchizaki Shinmei-sha}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11423430]] Wikidata name: Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[:en:1602|1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1881|1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[:en:July 21|July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[:en:1997|1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ōu Main Line]] [[:en:Tsuchizaki Station|Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki|Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha]] [[ja:土崎神明社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita (city)]] [[Category:History of Akita, Akita]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsuchizaki Shinmei-sha]][[ja:土崎神明社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11423430]] Wikidata name: Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[:en:1602|1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1881|1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[:en:July 21|July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[:en:1997|1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ōu Main Line]] [[:en:Tsuchizaki Station|Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki|Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee]
