Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[:en:1602|1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1881|1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[:en:July 21|July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[:en:1997|1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ōu Main Line]] [[:en:Tsuchizaki Station|Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki|Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1881|1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[:en:July 21|July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[:en:1997|1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ōu Main Line]] [[:en:Tsuchizaki Station|Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ōu Main Line]] [[:en:Tsuchizaki Station|Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station [[commons:Category:Tsuchizaki Shinmei-sha]][[ja:土崎神明社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsuchizaki Shinmei-sha]][[ja:土崎神明社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11423430]] Wikidata name: Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[:en:1602|1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1881|1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[:en:July 21|July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[:en:1997|1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ōu Main Line]] [[:en:Tsuchizaki Station|Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki|Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[:en:1602|1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1881|1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[:en:July 21|July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[:en:1997|1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ōu Main Line]] [[:en:Tsuchizaki Station|Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki|Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] The shrine's festival, held on [[:en:July 21|July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[:en:1997|1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ōu Main Line]] [[:en:Tsuchizaki Station|Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] * [[:en:Tsuchizaki|Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == [[co:commons:Category:Tsuchizaki Shinmei-sha]][[ja:土崎神明社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsuchizaki Shinmei-sha]][[ja:土崎神明社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsuchizaki Shinmei-sha]][[ja:土崎神明社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11423430]] Wikidata name: Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] to Akita in [[:en:1602|1602]]. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of [[:en:Minato_Castle|Dewa Minato Castle]] in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in [[:en:1881|1881]] and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on [[:en:July 21|July 21]], features the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]], which was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in [[:en:1997|1997]]. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the [[:en:Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession]] (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Ōu Main Line|Ōu Main Line]] [[:en:Tsuchizaki Station|Tsuchizaki Station]]. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}} to Akita in {{ill|1602|en|1602|WD=Q6727|lt=1602}}. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of {{ill|Minato_Castle|en|Minato_Castle|WD=Q11563138|lt=Dewa Minato Castle}} in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881}} and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21}}, features the {{ill|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|en|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|WD=Q7849923|lt=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession}}, which was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the {{ill|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|en|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|WD=Q7849923|lt=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession}} (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten Station|en|Tsuchizaki Station|WD=Q7849925|lt=Tsuchizaki Station}}. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of {{ill|Minato_Castle|en|Minato_Castle|WD=Q11563138|lt=Dewa Minato Castle|1=Minato Castle}} in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}} and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21|1=July 21}}, features the {{ill|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|en|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|WD=Q7849923|lt=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession|1=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Shrine Annual Celebration And The Float Festival}}, which was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997|1=1997}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the {{ill|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|en|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|WD=Q7849923|lt=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession|1=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Shrine Annual Celebration And The Float Festival}} (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Ōu Main Line|en|Ōu Main Line|WD=Q386495|lt=Ōu Main Line|1=Ōu Main Line}} {{ill|Tsuchizaki Station|en|Tsuchizaki Station|WD=Q7849925|lt=Tsuchizaki Station|1=Tsuchizaki Station}}. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * {{ill|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|en|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|WD=Q7849923|lt=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession|1=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Shrine Annual Celebration And The Float Festival}} * [[:en:Tsuchizaki|Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:土崎神明社.jpg|250px|Shrine Hall]]
Worship Hall | location = 3-9-37 Tsuchizaki Port Chuo, Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|39|45|25|N|140|04|15|E|scale:30000_region:JP-05_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha}} | deity = [[Amaterasu]] | shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620) | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] with copper roofing | festivals = July 21 (festival) | map = {{location map|Japan Akita|lat_deg=39|lat_min=45|lat_sec=25|lon_deg=140|lon_min=04|lon_sec=15|label=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha''' ({{nihongo|土崎神明社|つちざきしんめいしゃ}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Akita City, Akita Prefecture, Japan. It is a former [[Prefectural Shrine]] under the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} system and is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]], the sun goddess. == History == The shrine originated as the tutelary deity (''ujigami'') of Kawaguchi Sojiro, a retainer who accompanied [[Satake Yoshinobu]] from {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}} to Akita in {{ill|1602|en|1602|WD=Q6727|lt=1602|1=1602}}. In [[Genna period|Genna]] 6 (1620), the shrine was moved to its current location on the ruins of {{ill|Minato_Castle|en|Minato_Castle|WD=Q11563138|lt=Dewa Minato Castle|1=Minato Castle}} in Tsuchizaki, where it was established by Kawaguchi as a general guardian shrine of Tsuchizaki Port Town. It became a Prefectural Shrine in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}} and later joined the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] after World War II. The shrine's festival, held on {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21|1=July 21}}, features the {{ill|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|en|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|WD=Q7849923|lt=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession|1=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Shrine Annual Celebration And The Float Festival}}, which was designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] of Japan in {{ill|1997|en|1997|WD=Q2088|lt=1997|1=1997}}. == deity == The enshrined deity is [[Amaterasu]], the goddess of the sun and the universe. == festivals == * '''January 1''': New Year's festival, First Shinto Prayer (''Hatsumode'') * '''January 15''': ''Donto'' festival * '''February 3''': Setsubun festival * '''February 27''': Harvest Prayer festival (''Kinensai'') * '''Vernal Equinox Day''': Fire-Prevention festival (''Chinka-sai'') * '''June 30''': Great Purification (''Oharae'') * '''July 21''': annual Grand festival – Includes the {{ill|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|en|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|WD=Q7849923|lt=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession|1=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Shrine Annual Celebration And The Float Festival}} (July 20–21) * '''November 23''': Harvest festival (''Niinamesai'') * '''December 31''': Year-End Purification (''Oharae'') == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Ōkuninushi Shrine''' * '''Sugawara Shrine''' (Port Tenmangu Shrine) * '''Nishinomiya Shrine''' * '''Hikage Inari Shrine''' * '''Banjō Shrine''' == Cultural Properties == * '''Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession''' – Designated as an [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]]. * '''Calligraphy Plaque by Satake Yoshinaga''' – A handwritten dedication by the feudal lord. == Timeline == * '''1620 (Genna 6)''': Shrine established on the ruins of Dewa Minato Castle by Kawaguchi Sojiro. == Access == === By Train === * A 2-minute walk from {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Ōu Main Line|en|Ōu Main Line|WD=Q386495|lt=Ōu Main Line|1=Ōu Main Line}} {{ill|Tsuchizaki Station|en|Tsuchizaki Station|WD=Q7849925|lt=Tsuchizaki Station|1=Tsuchizaki Station}}. === By Bus === * A 4-minute walk from Tsuchizaki Station Entrance bus stop (accessible via routes from Akita Station, excluding Selion and Ferry Terminal lines). === Parking === * Parking is available. == References == * Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City Merger 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''History of Tsuchizaki – A Commemorative Volume for the Merger of Tsuchizaki Port Town and Akita City,'' 1992. * Akita Sakigake Shinpo Editorial Department, ''Akita Encyclopedia,'' Akita Sakigake Shinpo, 1981, ISBN 4870200074. == See Also == * [[Shinto]] ''' [[List of Shinto Shrines]] * {{ill|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|en|Tsuchizaki_Shinmeisha_Shrine_Annual_Celebration_And_The_Float_Festival|WD=Q7849923|lt=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha festival's Hikiyama Procession|1=Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha Shrine Annual Celebration And The Float Festival}} * [[:en:Tsuchizaki|Tsuchizaki]] * [[Tsuchizaki District Park]] == External Links == * [http://www.tsuchizakishinnmeisha.or.jp/ Official Website of Tsuchizaki Shinmeisha] * [http://hikiyama.akitalink.com/ Official Site of Tsuchizaki Port Hikiyama festival Committee] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|土崎神明社|version=89182105|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsuchizaki Shinmei-sha}} {{wikidata link|Q11423430|co|commons:Category:Tsuchizaki Shinmei-sha|ja|土崎神明社}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akita (city)]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Akita Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Akita, Akita]] [[Category:Ken-sha]]
